spec_match.rationale: The tree maps the resolved response plus AbortError, OperationError, NotSupportedError, and caller-misuse fall-through cases to merchant actions using real PaymentRequest probes where applicable.

interactive.rationale: Install and canMakePayment controls plus five outcome buttons now run real browser code, update the action card, and highlight the routed decision-tree node.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page promises that real browser results route through the tree, and the NotSupportedError and SecurityError-style branches are now runnable rather than static.

edge_cases.rationale: Unsupported payment methods and lost user activation are probed directly, while the generic branch still handles other setup or policy failures.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, paired button color/background overrides, and no raw hex color additions.

summary: The decision tree now lights up each major branch from runnable scenarios and includes a copyable if/else ladder that mirrors the visual route.
